subject: Vendor security questionnaire — 47 questions attached
body:
Dear Vendor Management,

As part of our annual third-party risk assessment, please find attached our security questionnaire (47 questions covering data handling, encryption, access controls, incident response, and business continuity). We require your completion of this within 10 business days.

In addition, please provide:
- Current SOC 2 Type II report
- Penetration test summary (last 12 months)
- Data flow diagram
- Sub-processor list

Kindly direct your responses to vendor-risk@apexholdings.com and CC me.

Regards,
Sarah Mitchell
Director, Information Security
Apex Holdings

subject: Request for redlined MSA review
body:
Legal has completed an initial review of your Master Services Agreement and prepared a redlined version for discussion.

Key points requiring negotiation:
- Section 4.2 (Limitation of Liability) — current cap unacceptable, propose 2x annual fees
- Section 7 (Indemnification) — needs mutual indemnification language
- Section 11 (Data Protection) — must reference our DPA terms verbatim
- Section 14 (Termination) — termination for convenience with 60-day notice required

Please confirm who should coordinate contract negotiations from your side and provide estimated turnaround. We are targeting a signed MSA by month-end.

David Chen
VP, Legal
Vertex Industries

subject: Multi-stakeholder review — procurement, legal, and IT
body:
CC: procurement@acme-corp.com, legal@acme-corp.com, it-governance@acme-corp.com

Dear Account Management,

We are consolidating feedback from several departments before final approval of our proposed contract. Please respond to the open questions regarding:

1. Support SLAs and escalation paths
2. Data residency commitments for EU-based users
3. Sub-processor change notification process
4. Data export procedures upon termination
5. Single Sign-On configuration support

Procurement requires confirmation of these items before issuing the PO. Please coordinate response across the team and respond by EOD Wednesday.

Regards,
Priya Kapoor
Senior Procurement Manager
Acme Corp

subject: Annual prepay — request for additional discount
body:
Dear Account Team,

We are reviewing our subscription for the upcoming renewal cycle. Our preference is to move to annual prepay (12-month commitment, full payment upfront) on the Business tier across our 240 seats.

Given the commitment level, we'd like to discuss additional discount beyond the standard annual rate. Comparable vendors offer 18-22% off list for similar commitments. We would like to see at least 15% to make this competitive against our other options under evaluation.

Please confirm what flexibility your team has on pricing for this tier.

Michael Bauer
Director, Procurement
Summit Global

subject: Pre-sale due diligence — please confirm
body:
Hello,

Before we sign, our team needs written confirmation on the following:

1. Data is encrypted at rest (AES-256) and in transit (TLS 1.2+)
2. Backup retention is at least 30 days
3. RTO and RPO commitments for disaster recovery
4. Whether sub-processors are notified of changes 30 days in advance
5. EU data residency option is available (no US data transfer)
6. SAML SSO support with Okta and Azure AD

These are non-negotiable for our Information Security team's approval. Please respond with specifics — vague answers won't pass review.

Laura Gomez
Security Architect
Northstar Enterprise

subject: Integration requirements — ServiceNow
body:
Dear Integration Team,

As part of our enterprise rollout, we require bidirectional integration with ServiceNow ITSM. Specifically:
- Tickets created in your platform should auto-create incidents in ServiceNow
- Incident updates in ServiceNow should sync back to your platform
- Custom field mapping (we have ~30 custom fields)
- SLA inheritance across systems

Is this supported natively, via API, or via a third-party connector? Please share documentation and estimated implementation effort.

Regards,
James Walker
Senior IT Architect
Acme Corp

subject: SSO/SAML setup — Okta configuration questions
body:
Hello,

We are setting up SAML SSO with our Okta tenant for a 380-user rollout. A few questions before we proceed:

1. Do you support IdP-initiated and SP-initiated SSO flows? Both required.
2. Group-based provisioning (SCIM 2.0) supported?
3. Custom attribute mapping (we need to pass department and cost center)?
4. Force-SSO option to disable password fallback once SAML is configured?

Please share the SAML metadata XML and provisioning guide so our IT team can begin configuration.

Anita Rao
Identity Management Lead
Vertex Industries

subject: DPA execution required — GDPR compliance
body:
Dear Account Manager,

Per our internal compliance requirements, we cannot transmit personal data of our EU-based users to your platform without an executed Data Processing Agreement.

Please provide your standard DPA or confirm willingness to sign ours. Either is acceptable. The agreement must include:
- Article 28 sub-processor language
- SCCs as applicable
- 72-hour breach notification commitment
- Right-to-audit clause

Provisioning is paused pending DPA execution.

Kind regards,
Robert King
Data Protection Officer
Summit Global

subject: Custom invoicing — PO-based billing required
body:
Hello,

Our finance department requires all vendor invoicing to be PO-based with NET-45 payment terms. The current self-serve credit-card billing flow is not acceptable for our enterprise procurement process.

Please confirm:
- PO-based invoicing is supported
- Invoices can be issued against a master PO with line-item references
- Custom billing address and entity name on invoices
- Quarterly invoicing option (vs monthly)

If any of these aren't standard, please advise on the path to enable them.

Emma Fischer
Accounts Payable Manager
Apex Holdings

subject: Audit log export — SOC 2 evidence collection
body:
Dear Support,

We are preparing for our SOC 2 Type II audit. The auditor has requested audit log evidence covering Q1 2026 for our workspace, specifically:

- All user authentication events (success and failure)
- Administrative actions (role changes, user invites/removals)
- Data access events on sensitive ticket categories
- API key creation/rotation events

The export must be in a tamper-evident format (signed logs preferred, otherwise SHA-256 manifest acceptable).

Please advise on export procedure and turnaround time.

Victor Alvarez
Compliance Manager
Vertex Industries

subject: Sub-processor change — formal notification request
body:
Dear Vendor,

We received your sub-processor change notification dated 18 February 2026 (new sub-processor: undisclosed cloud provider in Asia-Pacific region).

Per our DPA Section 7, we require:
1. Specific sub-processor identity (entity name, jurisdiction)
2. Data categories processed by this sub-processor
3. Security certifications held by sub-processor
4. Confirmation that SCCs are in place

Until we receive these details, we object to the addition of this sub-processor per our DPA rights.

Kindly respond within 14 days.

Helen Brooks
Legal Counsel, Privacy
Summit Global

subject: Renewal at 220 seats — pricing review
body:
Hi,

Our 12-month subscription is up for renewal on 7 July. We are currently at 220 seats on the Business tier and have grown 4x since signing.

Given the growth, I'd like to discuss:
- A multi-year commitment (2-3 year) in exchange for locked pricing
- Volume discount tiers at 250, 300, 500 seats
- Bundled access to your Enterprise features (SAML, audit log, IP allowlist) given our size

Can we schedule a call with someone empowered to negotiate? Procurement is engaged and we have budget approval.

Tom Richardson
VP, Customer Operations
Summit Global

subject: Data residency requirements — APAC region
body:
Dear Support,

Our APAC entity is required by local regulation (specifically Singapore PDPA and similar regional laws) to keep customer personal data within the APAC region. We cannot transmit this data to your US-based infrastructure.

Please confirm:
1. APAC data residency option is available
2. Which AWS region or equivalent is used
3. Whether our existing tenant can be migrated, or if we need a separate workspace
4. Pricing implications

This is a regulatory blocker; without APAC residency we cannot extend the contract to our Singapore subsidiary.

Mei Lin
Privacy Counsel — APAC
Summit Global

subject: Information security review — pen test results
body:
Dear Account Management,

As part of our annual vendor review, our Information Security team requires access to your most recent external penetration test summary (within last 12 months). Specifically:

- Scope of the test
- Methodology used
- Findings with severity ratings
- Remediation status of any High/Critical findings

We understand this is sensitive — happy to execute an NDA if not already in place.

Also of interest: bug bounty program details (if any) and your responsible disclosure policy URL.

Ahmed Hassan
Lead, Third-Party Risk Management
Summit Global

subject: RBAC configuration — custom roles required
body:
Hello,

Our deployment will require custom role definitions beyond your standard Admin/Editor/Viewer set. Specifically we need:

1. 'Audit Reader' — read-only access to audit logs only, no ticket data
2. 'Finance Reviewer' — access to billing-tagged tickets only
3. 'Customer Success' — access to assigned customer tickets, not org-wide

Is custom role creation supported via API or admin UI? If not natively supported, what is the workaround for similar role separation?

Sophie Laurent
Senior IT Architect
Acme Corp

subject: BAA execution — HIPAA workload
body:
Dear Vendor,

Our use case includes processing of protected health information (PHI) under HIPAA. Per regulation, we require a Business Associate Agreement (BAA) to be executed prior to onboarding.

Please provide:
1. Your standard BAA template, or willingness to sign ours
2. Confirmation that your platform is HIPAA-eligible for relevant features
3. List of HIPAA-restricted features (if any)
4. Encryption and access control evidence for PHI handling

We cannot proceed without this. Kindly route to your privacy/compliance team for response.

Daniel Moore
Director, Healthcare IT
Acme Corp

subject: Custom SLA — uptime and response time commitments
body:
Dear Account Team,

Our enterprise agreement requires custom SLA commitments beyond your standard terms. Specifically:

- Platform uptime: 99.95% measured monthly (vs your standard 99.5%)
- P1 ticket response: 30 minutes 24x7 (vs your 1 hour business hours)
- P2 ticket response: 4 hours 24x7
- Service credit schedule for SLA misses

These commitments are aligned with what we offer our own customers. Without these, we cannot deploy your platform in our customer-facing workflow.

Please confirm feasibility and any pricing implications.

Rachel Green
VP, Vendor Management
Vertex Industries

subject: IP allowlisting — required for our security policy
body:
Hello,

Our Information Security policy requires that any administrative access to vendor platforms be restricted by IP address (corporate VPN ranges only). End-user access (regular agents) is acceptable from any IP.

Does your platform support:
1. IP allowlisting at the admin role level?
2. Multiple allowed CIDR ranges per workspace?
3. Logging of attempted access from disallowed IPs?

If available, please share configuration documentation. If not natively supported, advise on roadmap or workaround.

Arjun Mehta
Information Security Officer
Apex Holdings

subject: Termination assistance — data export commitments
body:
Dear Vendor,

As we work towards contract signature, our legal team requires explicit language around termination assistance. Specifically:

1. Upon termination, we receive a full export of our data within 30 days, in machine-readable format (JSON or CSV)
2. A 60-day post-termination access period for retrieval and migration
3. Confirmation that data is permanently deleted from your systems 90 days after termination
4. Written attestation of the deletion

Please confirm whether these terms are acceptable. If not standard, advise on negotiable language.

Olivia Scott
Senior Counsel
Vertex Industries

subject: Workday HRIS integration — provisioning automation
body:
Hello,

We are evaluating your platform for a 450-user deployment. A blocker for our IT team is automated user provisioning from our Workday HRIS.

Do you support:
1. SCIM 2.0 from Workday
2. Custom attribute sync (department, manager, cost center)
3. Auto-deprovisioning on Workday termination
4. Just-in-time provisioning on first SAML login

Without this we cannot manage 450 users manually. Please share documentation or roadmap.

Wei Zhang
Senior IAM Engineer
Acme Corp

subject: Risk acceptance — list of accepted findings from your SOC 2
body:
Hi,

We've reviewed your SOC 2 Type II report. Most findings are acceptable to our risk team, but we'd like to discuss:

1. CC6.1 partial finding — clarification on how compensating controls were assessed
2. CC7.2 finding — current remediation status and timeline
3. CC8.1 — confirmation that the corrective action plan was completed

We accept residual risk for items not listed above. Please confirm so we can close out our review.

Natalie Reed
Third-Party Risk Analyst
Apex Holdings

subject: Custom reporting — quarterly business reviews
body:
Dear Account Manager,

As part of our enterprise contract, we expect quarterly business review meetings with custom reporting:

- Ticket volume and SLA performance per customer cohort
- Agent utilization across our 12 teams
- Customer satisfaction trends with statistical significance
- Year-over-year benchmarks against our industry peers (if available)

Which of these can be delivered out-of-the-box? Which require custom build? What is the standard QBR cadence for accounts of our size?

George Hill
Director, Customer Operations
Acme Corp

subject: Single-tenant deployment — feasibility and cost
body:
Hello,

Our Information Security team has flagged multi-tenant SaaS as a concern for our regulated workload. We'd like to explore a single-tenant deployment option:

1. Is single-tenant (or dedicated infrastructure) available?
2. Pricing premium for this option
3. Performance and feature parity with multi-tenant
4. Implementation timeline

If single-tenant isn't offered, please explain the alternative isolation guarantees in your standard offering.

Maria Costa
Chief Information Security Officer
Northstar Enterprise

subject: Custom domain (CNAME) — branding requirements
body:
Hi,

We require a custom domain configuration for our deployment. Specifically:

1. Customer-facing chat widget served from chat.summit-global.com (CNAME)
2. Email replies sent from support@summit-global.com (DKIM via your DNS)
3. Status page integrated into our internal Confluence

Is this offered? If yes, share configuration guides. If no, advise on roadmap or workaround.

Kevin Turner
Senior IT Manager
Summit Global

subject: Pricing — request for ROI analysis materials
body:
Dear Account Team,

We are in the final stages of evaluating your platform against two competitors. Our finance committee has requested a customer-specific ROI analysis to support the purchase decision.

Can you provide:
1. Comparable enterprise customer case studies (similar industry/size)
2. Average time-to-value benchmarks
3. Total cost of ownership calculator (3-year)
4. References from customers we can speak with

Decision committee meets in 11 days. Materials needed by EOW.

Lisa Young
Director, Vendor Strategy
Summit Global

subject: Vendor onboarding — required documentation
body:
Hello,

To complete vendor onboarding in our procurement system, please provide:

1. W-9 (or local tax equivalent)
2. Certificate of insurance — general liability and cyber
3. Banking details on company letterhead
4. Diversity certification (if applicable)
5. ESG / sustainability disclosure (we ask all vendors)

Kindly route to your finance/operations team. Onboarding is the gate to PO issuance.

John Patel
Vendor Onboarding Specialist
Vertex Industries

subject: Disaster recovery — request for DR plan documentation
body:
Dear Vendor,

Our business continuity team requires a copy of your disaster recovery and business continuity plan, including:

1. RTO and RPO commitments
2. Backup procedures and retention
3. Geographic redundancy details
4. Failover testing frequency and last test results
5. Communication protocols during incidents

This is part of our ISO 22301 compliance program. Please respond within 15 business days.

Claire Evans
Manager, Business Continuity
Northstar Enterprise

subject: Procurement and governance review
body:
Dear Team,

We are currently evaluating your platform as part of an enterprise procurement and governance review. Please provide the requested information and expected timelines for completion. This evaluation determines whether we proceed to contract negotiation.

Items requested:
- ISO 27001 certificate (current)
- Recent SOC 2 Type II report (under NDA)
- GDPR compliance attestation
- Reference customers in financial services (3-5)
- Implementation timeline estimate for 600-seat rollout

Kindly respond within 7 business days.

Eric Nelson
Procurement Lead
Vertex Industries

subject: Reference call request — 3 customer references
body:
Hello,

As we approach contract decision, our committee has asked for 3 customer references we can speak to directly. Preferably:

- 1 in financial services
- 1 with similar deployment size (400+ seats)
- 1 who has been a customer 2+ years

We'll keep calls under 30 minutes and provide questions in advance. Can you facilitate?

Monica Cruz
Senior Procurement Analyst
Apex Holdings

subject: Service catalog inclusion — request for vendor profile
body:
Dear Account Manager,

Following contract approval, we will list your platform in our internal service catalog. Please provide:

1. Service description (200 words max)
2. Use case scenarios (3-4 bullet points)
3. Onboarding process summary
4. Support contact for end users
5. Cost per seat (for chargeback purposes)

This enables our 8,000-employee org to request access through standard internal channels.

Brian Lee
IT Service Management
Summit Global

subject: Compliance — quarterly attestation requirement
body:
Hi,

As part of our vendor management program, we require quarterly attestation from key vendors. Attached is our Q1 2026 attestation form covering:

- Continued SOC 2 compliance
- No material changes to data handling practices
- No undisclosed breaches in the reporting period
- Sub-processor list update
- Updated insurance certificates

Response due 30 days from receipt. Counterparty: third-party-risk@apexholdings.com.

Nina Shah
Third-Party Risk Officer
Apex Holdings

subject: Integration with internal SIEM — log streaming
body:
Hello,

Our Security Operations Center requires audit log streaming from your platform into our internal SIEM (Splunk). Specifically:

1. Real-time log streaming (not batch export)
2. Structured log format (CEF or JSON)
3. Event types: auth, admin actions, data access, API calls
4. Configurable filtering at source

Is this supported? If yes, what's the configuration process? If no, advise on roadmap.

Peter Novak
Senior SOC Engineer
Northstar Enterprise

subject: Custom legal terms — limitation of liability
body:
Dear Legal Counsel,

Reviewing your standard MSA — the limitation of liability cap at 'fees paid in prior 12 months' is below our standard threshold. Our policy requires either:

1. 2x annual fees, OR
2. Removal of cap for breach of confidentiality, data protection, IP, and indemnification obligations

Option 2 is preferable. Please confirm whether this is negotiable. If not, we cannot proceed.

Julia Martin
General Counsel
Acme Corp

subject: Custom training requirements — admin certification
body:
Hi,

For our deployment, we'd like to formally certify a small group of internal admins (4-5 people) on your platform. Question:

1. Do you offer formal admin certification training?
2. Is it instructor-led or self-paced?
3. Cost per attendee
4. Recertification requirements

This enables us to internally support our user base without escalating to your support for everything.

Chris Wood
IT Training Manager
Apex Holdings

subject: Annual security review — kickoff meeting request
body:
Dear Vendor Management,

As part of our annual third-party security review, we'd like to schedule a 60-minute call with your security team. Agenda:

1. Update on security program (since last year's review)
2. Walk-through of any new sub-processors or data flows
3. Discussion of recent incidents (if any)
4. Review of remediation items from last year

Proposed date: in next 3 weeks. Attendees from our side: CISO, Privacy Counsel, IT Director.

Angela Price
Manager, Vendor Security
Summit Global

subject: RFP response — your participation requested
body:
Hello,

We are conducting a formal RFP for our customer support platform. Your platform is one of 4 finalists.

Attached:
- RFP document (87 questions)
- Scoring methodology
- Timeline (responses due in 21 days)
- Decision committee members (for context)

Please confirm your team's ability to respond and assign a single point of contact for the RFP process.

Rohan Verma
Sourcing Manager
Acme Corp

subject: Custom workflow approval — change advisory board
body:
Hi,

Our Change Advisory Board has approved the rollout of your platform contingent on the following customizations being available:

1. Approval workflows for high-value customer escalations (manager sign-off required)
2. Mandatory commenting on certain status transitions (audit trail requirement)
3. Role-based visibility on PHI-tagged tickets (HIPAA workload)

Please confirm support and any configuration limitations.

Karen White
IT Project Manager
Summit Global

subject: European entity — separate contract required
body:
Hello,

Our German subsidiary requires a separate contract with your EU entity (if applicable) for tax and data residency reasons. The contract for our US parent does not cover our European operations.

Please confirm:
1. You have an EU entity for contracting
2. Currency options (EUR preferred)
3. Local jurisdiction (Germany or general EU)
4. Tax compliance (VAT handling, OSS scheme)

If you don't have an EU entity, advise on the path forward.

Stefan Koch
Legal Counsel, EU Operations
Acme Corp

subject: Conditional approval — penetration test required
body:
Dear Vendor,

Our InfoSec committee has conditionally approved your platform pending one additional item: we require a current penetration test summary from a reputable third party (NCC, Bishop Fox, or equivalent) within the past 6 months.

If your current report is older than 6 months, please indicate when the next test is scheduled.

We'd prefer to close out approval this quarter — please respond promptly.

Isabelle Roy
Governance and Compliance Lead
Apex Holdings

subject: TPRM tool integration — automated assessment exchange
body:
Hello,

Our Third-Party Risk Management tool is OneTrust. We'd like to streamline our annual reviews by exchanging assessment data via the OneTrust shared profile mechanism.

Does your team:
1. Have a published OneTrust profile?
2. Maintain an SCA / CAIQ self-assessment that can be shared?
3. Use any standardized assessment framework (e.g., SIG, CCM)?

This would save both teams substantial annual effort.

Mark Hughes
TPRM Program Director
Vertex Industries

subject: Custom SSO integration — non-standard IdP
body:
Hi,

We use a non-standard internal identity provider (proprietary, OIDC-compliant). Standard SAML may not work with it directly.

Does your platform support:
1. OIDC (not just SAML)?
2. Custom claim mapping?
3. Multiple IdPs per workspace (for federation)?

If only SAML is supported, can we use a third-party broker (like Auth0)?

Teresa Bell
Identity Engineering
Apex Holdings

subject: Implementation services — scope and pricing
body:
Hello,

We expect implementation to require significant migration assistance from your team. Specifically:

1. Migration from existing Zendesk instance (~120K historical tickets)
2. Custom integration build with our internal CRM
3. User training rollout across 4 global offices
4. Go-live support (2 weeks of dedicated engineer)

What does your professional services team offer for engagements of this size? Pricing?

Vikram Singh
Program Manager
Acme Corp

subject: API limits at enterprise scale — clarification needed
body:
Dear Account Team,

Reviewing your API documentation, we have concerns about rate limits for our scale. We anticipate ~50,000 API calls per hour at peak from our 380 agents.

Please clarify:
1. Default rate limits per workspace
2. Limits per API token vs per workspace
3. Burst capacity allowances
4. Process to request higher limits
5. Cost (if any) for elevated limits

We cannot proceed without confidence our usage pattern won't get throttled.

Grace Miller
Technical Lead, Integrations
Acme Corp

subject: ESG compliance — request for sustainability report
body:
Dear Vendor,

As part of our ESG vendor assessment, we require disclosures aligned with our internal sustainability program:

1. Carbon emissions disclosure (Scope 1, 2, 3)
2. Renewable energy commitments
3. DEI metrics for your workforce
4. Modern slavery statement (UK / Australia compliance)

This assessment is mandated for all vendors with >€100K annual spend. Please respond by quarter-end.

Leo Wagner
ESG Reporting Analyst
Northstar Enterprise

subject: Insurance — proof of coverage required
body:
Hello,

Per our standard vendor onboarding, please provide certificates of insurance for:

1. Commercial General Liability — minimum $5M per occurrence
2. Professional Liability / Errors & Omissions — minimum $10M
3. Cyber Liability — minimum $25M
4. Workers' Compensation (statutory limits)

Certificates should name Northstar Enterprise as additional insured where applicable. Please route to your finance/risk team.

Paula Diaz
Risk Management Coordinator
Northstar Enterprise

subject: Quarterly executive review — request for participation
body:
Dear Account Manager,

As a top-tier customer, we'd like to formalize quarterly executive reviews with your senior leadership. Suggested attendees from your side: VP of Customer Success and CTO. From ours: CIO and VP IT.

Proposed cadence: every 90 days, 60-min calls, alternating focus (strategic roadmap one quarter, operational health the next).

Please confirm interest and your team's availability.

Henry Ford
Director, Strategic Vendors
Apex Holdings

subject: Subprocessor approval — change pending
body:
Hi,

Your sub-processor change notification from 14 March 2026 (addition of unnamed AI services provider) requires approval from our Data Protection Officer before we can accept.

Questions before approval:
1. Which specific AI services provider? OpenAI, Anthropic, others?
2. What data is sent to them?
3. Is the data anonymized/pseudonymized before transmission?
4. Where is the provider's processing geographically located?
5. Can we opt out of this sub-processor while maintaining the rest of the service?

Pending approval, please confirm our data is NOT yet being sent to this sub-processor.

Elena Petrova
Data Protection Officer
Acme Corp

subject: Vendor diversity supplier program — disclosure form
body:
Dear Vendor,

As part of our supplier diversity initiative, we collect disclosure from all vendors:

1. Are you a certified diverse supplier (MBE, WBE, VBE, LGBTQ+)?
2. Approximate percentage of leadership team identifying as underrepresented
3. Diversity hiring goals (publicly stated)

This is voluntary but tracked. Responses inform our internal diversity scorecard.

Noah Carter
Supplier Diversity Coordinator
Acme Corp

subject: Mid-term contract amendment — additional seats
body:
Hello,

Our usage has grown faster than projected. We need to add 80 seats mid-term to our existing contract. Please confirm:

1. Pro-rated pricing for the remainder of our annual term
2. Whether the per-seat rate at our existing tier still applies, or if we trigger Enterprise pricing
3. Co-terming with our existing renewal date
4. Amendment paperwork required

We'd like to execute this within 2 weeks if possible.

Alicia Brown
Senior Procurement Specialist
Vertex Industries

subject: Enterprise contract — finalize and sign
body:
Dear Account Team,

Our committee has approved your platform for procurement. All due diligence complete. Final steps:

1. Final MSA execution (DocuSign acceptable)
2. Order form for 410 seats — Business tier, 24-month commitment
3. Provisioning timeline for our 6 global offices (staggered rollout)
4. Kickoff call with implementation team
5. Issuance of PO (we'll handle on receipt of countersigned MSA)

Please send the final agreement documents to legal@apexholdings.com (CC me). Looking forward to a productive partnership.

Omar Khalid
Director of Procurement
Apex Holdings
